Turbine Engines.q: What are the two principal types of compressors currently used in turbine engines?
Turbine Engines.a: Centrifugal flow and axial flow.

Turbine Engines.q: What is the purpose of interconnect tubes between CAN combustion chambers?
Turbine Engines.a: To propagate flame during starting to chambers without igniters.

Turbine Engines.q: What prevents an APU from exceeding maximum exhaust gas temperature when heavily loaded?
Turbine Engines.a: A load control valve modulates pneumatic load to keep temperatures within limits.

Turbine Engines.q: What are two ways of relieving thermal stress on a turbine disc?
Turbine Engines.a: Directing cooling air onto the face of the disc and using grooves/notches conforming to blade roots.

Turbine Engines.q: Name the types of turbine engine combustion chambers.
Turbine Engines.a: CAN, annular, CAN-annular, and reverse flow annular.

Turbine Engines.q: How is an APU shut down and why is this procedure used?
Turbine Engines.a: After unloading bleed air, it runs for a cool-down (≈3 min) to stabilize EGT and prevent thermal shock damage.

Turbine Engines.q: What is the prime factor in gas turbine engine performance?
Turbine Engines.a: Thermal efficiency.

Turbine Engines.q: What powers the variable inlet guide vanes on some APUs?
Turbine Engines.a: Fuel pressure.

Turbine Engines.q: What is the normal turbine engine starting sequence?
Turbine Engines.a: Rotate compressor with starter → turn ignition on → open fuel valve.

Turbine Engines.q: How long is a typical APU cool-down period?
Turbine Engines.a: 3 minutes.

Turbine Engines.q: Name the major sections of a gas turbine engine.
Turbine Engines.a: Air inlet, compressor, combustion, turbine, exhaust, gearbox, and accessory section.

Turbine Engines.q: What materials may be used to mark turbine engine parts?
Turbine Engines.a: Felt tip markers, layout die, or chalk.

Turbine Engines.q: Why must turbine blades be reinstalled in the same location?
Turbine Engines.a: To maintain turbine wheel balance.

Turbine Engines.q: Name compressor blade damages commonly found during inspection.
Turbine Engines.a: Dents, galling, pitting, burrs, burns, scratches, cracks, and gouges.

Turbine Engines.q: What type of mounting bolts/nuts should be used to mount an engine?
Turbine Engines.a: Always those recommended by the manufacturer.

Turbine Engines.q: If the engine lights off but doesn’t accelerate to idle RPM, what is this condition called?
Turbine Engines.a: A hung start.

Turbine Engines.q: What are the two major turbine failure classifications?
Turbine Engines.a: Thermodynamic and mechanical.

Turbine Engines.q: In general, what causes most thermodynamic failures?
Turbine Engines.a: Excessive bleed/leakage, ice, or faulty controls causing stall or overfueling.

Turbine Engines.q: What are the types of turbine blades?
Turbine Engines.a: Impulse, reaction, and reaction-impulse blades.

Turbine Engines.q: What is a result of high EGT, low RPM, and high fuel flow at all settings?
Turbine Engines.a: Loss of turbine efficiency.

Turbine Engines.q: What must be done to compressor bleed air before it’s used for cabin pressurization?
Turbine Engines.a: It must be cooled by an air conditioning unit.

Turbine Engines.q: An APU is what type of turbine engine?
Turbine Engines.a: A turboshaft engine.

Turbine Engines.q: What is the recommended stabilization time during trim procedures?
Turbine Engines.a: 5 minutes.

Turbine Engines.q: What checks are performed during engine trimming?
Turbine Engines.a: Idle RPM, max RPM, acceleration, and deceleration.

Turbine Engines.q: What readings are used to compute turbine discharge pressure/engine pressure ratio?
Turbine Engines.a: Ambient air temperature and barometric pressure.

Turbine Engines.q: What is the primary function of the compressor section?
Turbine Engines.a: Supply sufficient air at pressure to combustion burners.

Turbine Engines.q: What is a secondary function of the compressor section?
Turbine Engines.a: Provide bleed air for engine and aircraft purposes.

Turbine Engines.q: What repairs are permitted in the field on turbine fuel controls?
Turbine Engines.a: Only replacement and limited adjustments (idle RPM & max speed trimming).

Turbine Engines.q: High bypass engine fan blades are made of what materials?
Turbine Engines.a: Hollow titanium or composites.

Turbine Engines.q: What are the two common turbine fuel nozzle mounting methods?
Turbine Engines.a: Internally mounted or externally mounted.

Turbine Engines.q: Name the main components of a typical APU.
Turbine Engines.a: Small power turbine, bleed air system, and electrical generator.

Turbine Engines.q: Why are air blast nozzles used?
Turbine Engines.a: To improve mixing of fuel and airflow for optimum spray.

Turbine Engines.q: What are the basic elements of the accessory section?
Turbine Engines.a: Accessory case with mounting pads and gear train inside.

Turbine Engines.q: What are the three basic groups of turbine engine fuel controls?
Turbine Engines.a: Hydromechanical, hydromechanical/electronic, and FADEC.

Turbine Engines.q: What are the most important factors affecting turbine engine life?
Turbine Engines.a: EGT, engine speed, and cycles (takeoffs/landings).

Turbine Engines.q: What may indicate a turbojet/turbofan engine is out of trim?
Turbine Engines.a: High EGT at target EPR for takeoff power.

Turbine Engines.q: What effect does relative humidity have on turbojet/turbofan power?
Turbine Engines.a: Negligible effect.

Turbine Engines.q: How is bypass ratio calculated?
Turbine Engines.a: Ratio of fan bypass airflow (lbs/sec) to core airflow (lbs/sec).

Turbine Engines.q: What safety precautions must be taken before hooking a hoist onto an engine sling?
Turbine Engines.a: Recheck aircraft tail supports and wheel chocks.
